Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOs
The volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them.
Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing transportation costs.

articles.title: ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards
articles.description:
Delegates at event in Cape Verde highlight opportunities from tech while stressing AI is no replacement for talent
Last July, the Nigerian singer-songwriter Fave found herself caught up in a viral moment: an unauthorised version of a track featuring an AI choir had been released, quickly becoming an internet sensation. To get ahead of the situation, she recorded her own remix that integrated the AI-assisted song and added it to her discography.
“In my view, [that] was smart and very business aware,” Oyinkansola Fawehinmi, a Lagos-based entertainment lawyer, observed a few months later. “She essentially reclaimed the ‘AI version’ and released it as her own official expression.”
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
articles.description:
Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties
Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents.
It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours while completely surrounded and vastly outnumbered. It also alleged, without providing evidence, that the militants had been trained by European mercenary instructors, including Ukrainians. The casualty toll was not specified.

articles.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
articles.description:
Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East
Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry.
The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the Maritime Security Centre Indian Ocean (MSCIO), the tracking service of the EU’s naval force.

articles.title: Taking power in Mali might be a stretch but insurgents can force hand of weakened regime
articles.description:
Coordinated attack by JNIM and the Tuareg minority inflicted significant casualties on government forces and Russian auxiliaries
When al-Qaida-affiliated Islamic militants launched a series of attacks on military bases and raids into major towns in Mali and neighbouring Burkina Faso last summer, observers suggested they had been inspired by their counterparts in Syria, who had overthrown the regime of Bashar al-Assad and taken power six months or so earlier.
Despite the tactical successes that earned them the fearful title of the “Ghost Army”, seizing swathes of territory and denying cities and the military of fuel and other essentials, the chances of Jama’at Nusrat ul-Islam wa al-Muslimin (JNIM) definitively defeating Mali’s military regime and the thousand or so Russian mercenaries hired to defend it looked poor.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: US supreme court to hear whether protected status of Haitians and Syrians can be revoked
articles.description:
Case looks at whether Trump administration has authority to strip hundreds of thousands of immigrants of TPS
The supreme court will hear oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of Syrians and Haitians, under a program that has protected them from deportation due to safety concerns in their home countries.
People with TPS are given the permission to live and work in the US because the government has deemed their home countries to be unsafe due to war, political instability or natural disasters. In the past year, the Trump administration has attempted to cut the program for various countries, opening the door to the removal of hundreds of thousands of protected immigrants in the US.
 Continue reading...

The animals – herbivores native to sub-Saharan Africa – were originally imported into Colombia by the drug kingpin for his own entertainment. But the beasts and their offspring were left to roam free after his death in 1993.

The US army soldier charged with winning $400,000 by using insider information to bet on the removal of the ousted Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro pleaded not guilty to fraud charges on Tuesday.
Gannon Ken Van Dyke, 38, entered the plea in US district judge Margaret Garnett’s courtroom in Manhattan. Van Dyke sported a shaved head and wore a black blazer, jeans and brown shoes as he arrived to the courtroom with his lawyers, Zach Intrater and Mark Geragos.

The Mexican authorities have arrested two top criminals, one of them a close ally of the slain founder of the Jalisco New Generation cartel (CJNG), prompting gunmen to block roads in the western state of Nayarit.
Audias Flores, known as “El Jardinero”, is a regional commander in control of swathes of CJNG territory along Mexico’s Pacific coast. He was considered a potential successor to Nemesio Oseguera, alias “El Mencho”, who ran the cartel and was killed in a security operation in February.

Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexico was not aware of US participation until four officials were killed in car crash
Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, said on Monday that her government told the United States, in a diplomatic note, that the unauthorized presence of US officials at an anti-narcotics operation in the northern state of Chihuahua should not be repeated.
The incident came to light after two US officials, along with two Mexican officials, were killed in a car crash on 19 April after the operation. Sheinbaum has said the federal government was not aware of the participation of the US officials, who were widely reported to be CIA officers.
 Continue reading...

articles.titleJa: メキシコ、米捜査官の事故死は許可なき活動だったと発表
articles.summary: 麻薬ラボ破壊作戦の後、米CIA職員とされる2人の米国人が事故死。メキシコは彼らが現地での活動許可を得ていなかったと発表。

articles.title: Rights groups critical as Venezuela prisoner release scheme 'coming to an end'
articles.description: More than 500 political prisoners are thought to still be in jail, despite the releases since the amnesty law was brought in.
articles.titleJa: ベネズエラの政治犯釈放計画「終幕へ」、人権団体は批判
articles.summary: 恩赦法導入後、多くの政治犯が釈放されたが、500人以上が依然として投獄されているとみられる。釈放計画の終了に人権団体は批判的。

articles.title: The other life of US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal
articles.description: The Army officer charged with using classified information to win $400,000 is also a real estate investor with rave reviews on Airbnb.
articles.titleJa: マドゥロ大統領解任への賭けで告発された米兵のもう一つの顔
articles.summary: 機密情報を使って40万ドルを稼いだとされる米陸軍将校は、Airbnbで高評価を得ている不動産投資家でもある。

articles.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
articles.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.
articles.titleJa: 歴史を作り、ネイマールと対戦 - リンガード、ブラジルでの生活を語る
articles.summary: ブラジル移籍後初の主要インタビューで、ジェシー・リンガードが新生活とマンチェスター・ユナイテッドについてBBCに語る。

articles.title: Voice notes are massive in some countries but not the UK - here's why
articles.description: Voice notes have they taken some countries by storm, while failing to truly take off in Britain.
articles.titleJa: ボイスメッセージは一部の国で大流行、英国ではなぜか？
articles.summary: ボイスメッセージは一部の国で爆発的な人気を博しているが、英国では定着していない理由を探る。

articles.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
articles.description: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.
articles.titleJa: '一緒に泣いた': トランプ氏の国外追放強化、夫婦に苦渋の決断
articles.summary: 不法移民の配偶者は、離別か新天地への移住かの苦渋の選択を迫られている。

articles.title: Brazil: A warning on how AI and deepfakes can become an ’excessive risk‘ to women and girls
articles.description: A technical note by research center Internetlab points out that advances in synthetic image creators increase the risks of gender violence online and talks about platforms’ accountability
articles.titleJa: ブラジル：AIとディープフェイクが女性と少女にとって「過剰なリスク」になる可能性についての警告
articles.summary: AIとディープフェイクの進歩がジェンダー暴力のリスクを高め、プラットフォームの責任について論じられています。

articles.title: AI hype narrative reaches the public healthcare system in El Salvador
articles.description: This time we take you to El Salvador, examining government claims that an AI-supported transformation of the public healthcare system will make it the most advanced in the world.
articles.titleJa: AIへの期待がエルサルバドルの公衆衛生システムに到達
articles.summary: AI支援による公衆衛生システムの変革が、世界で最も先進的なシステムになると政府が主張しています。

articles.title: The technological transformation of Colombia's armed conflict
articles.description: More than simply a uniform adoption of artificial intelligence, a technological innovation has emerged that is transforming the battlefield, decision-making processes, local narratives, and forms of social control.
articles.titleJa: コロンビアの武力紛争の技術的変革
articles.summary: AIの採用だけでなく、技術革新が戦場、意思決定、社会統制のあり方を変化させています。

articles.title: When dry season never ends
articles.description: In an era marked by intense transnational mobility, the uneven distribution of rights and experiences in host communities reflects underlying dynamics of power and privilege that demand further scrutiny.
articles.titleJa: 乾季が終わらないとき
articles.summary: 国際的な移動が激しい時代、受け入れコミュニティにおける権利と経験の不均等な分配は、さらなる調査を必要とする力関係と特権の力学を反映しています。

articles.title: Community micro-hydropower plants revolutionise isolated areas of the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Community micro-hydropower projects in the DR rely on local leadership to deliver energy from renewable sources to isolated areas excluded from national distribution systems, contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions.
articles.titleJa: コミュニティ小水力発電所がドミニカ共和国の孤立地域に革命をもたらす
articles.summary: 再生可能エネルギー源からの電力供給により、温室効果ガス排出削減に貢献しています。

articles.title: How Bad Bunny brought the issue of Puerto Rico’s power grid into world view
articles.description: Puerto Rico’s electricity system has faced repeated crises since 2017’s Hurricane Maria, which destroyed much of the grid and triggered the longest blackout in modern U.S. history; outages remain routine.
articles.titleJa: バッド・バニーがプエルトリコの電力網問題を世界に知らしめた方法
articles.summary: ハリケーン・マリア以降、プエルトリコの電力システムは危機に直面し、停電が日常化しています。

articles.title: Ecuador’s Netanyahu and Trump supporters target Ecuadorian–Iranian Cultural Center
articles.description: Wars no longer have defined frontlines. Battlefields can be anywhere those in power decide that a neighbor, due to their faith, ethnicity, or background, is an enemy.
articles.titleJa: エクアドルのネタニヤフとトランプ支持者がエクアドル・イラン文化センターを標的に
articles.summary: 権力者が敵と見なした隣人を標的にする、国境の曖昧な紛争について論じています。

articles.title: Concerns grow as Jamaica ends Cuban medical programme
articles.description: Terminating the decades-old programme that brought hundreds of Cuban medical personnel to its shores is an unusual move, especially for a programme that had been running smoothly for 50 years.
articles.titleJa: ジャマイカがキューバの医療プログラムを終了し懸念が高まる
articles.summary: 50年間順調に運営されてきたキューバからの医療人材受け入れプログラムを終了することは、異例の動きです。

articles.title: From menstrual dignity to digital safety: How grassroots feminists are redefining gender justice
articles.description: What connects a pad box in a Nigerian school, a coalition meeting in Pakistan, and a digital security workshop in Paraguay? They each address a structural barrier that limits women’s participation.
articles.titleJa: 月経の尊厳からデジタルセキュリティまで：草の根フェミニストがいかにジェンダー正義を再定義するか
articles.summary: 学校の生理用品ボックス、パキスタンの連合会議、パラグアイのデジタルセキュリティワークショップが、女性の参加を制限する構造的障壁に対処しています。

articles.title: Democracy needs women: Feminist leadership in times of shrinking enabling environments for civil society
articles.description: When feminist leadership expands, democracy deepens. It becomes more accountable, inclusive, and participatory. Women’s civic participation is not about political correctness but about democratic survival.
articles.titleJa: 民主主義は女性を必要とする：市民社会のための協力環境が縮小する時代におけるフェミニストのリーダーシップ
articles.summary: フェミニストのリーダーシップの拡大は、民主主義の深化、説明責任、包摂性、参加性を高めます。

articles.title: Flying cars and 5-minute charges: China's EV industry sees opportunity in Iran crisis
articles.description: As the US-Iran has sent the world scrambling for fuel, China is positioned to benefit from its rapid shift into electric vehicles.
articles.titleJa: 空飛ぶ車と5分充電：中国EV産業、イラン危機に商機を見出す
articles.summary: イラン危機による燃料需要の変動を受け、中国のEV産業が新たな機会を捉えようとしている。

articles.title: Australian mother who faked son's cancer to fund lavish lifestyle jailed
articles.description: The mother-of-two shaved her six-year-old's son's head and eyebrows as part of the deception.
articles.titleJa: 息子の癌を偽り贅沢な生活を送ったオーストラリアの母親、投獄される
articles.summary: 2児の母が、6歳の息子にがんを患わせたように見せかけ、寄付金を詐取した罪で有罪判決を受けた。

articles.title: A faraway conflict threatens livelihoods in India's glass hub
articles.description: Supply disruptions due to Middle East tensions have pushed up costs in India’s glass industry, putting workers at risk.
articles.titleJa: 遠い紛争がインドのガラス産業の生活を脅かす
articles.summary: 中東情勢による供給網の混乱がインドのガラス産業のコストを押し上げ、労働者を危険にさらしている。

articles.title: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
articles.description: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.
articles.titleJa: 日本からの抗議を受け、NZが第二次世界大戦の慰安婦像設置計画を中止
articles.summary: 日本政府は、オークランドでの慰安婦像設置が外交関係を損なう可能性があると警告していた。

articles.title: BBC reports from scene of fatal Indonesia train crash
articles.description: At least 15 people have died after a train crashed into the female-only carriage of a commuter train in Bekasi.
articles.titleJa: インドネシアで発生した列車衝突事故、BBCが現場から報道
articles.summary: ベカシで発生した列車同士の衝突事故により、少なくとも15人が死亡した。

articles.title: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
articles.description: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.
articles.titleJa: 日本航空、地上ハンドラーとしてヒューマノイドロボットを試験導入
articles.summary: 将来的には、客室清掃や地上支援機器の操作にロボットが役立つ可能性がある。

articles.title: Executions in North Korea ramped up significantly during pandemic - report
articles.description: Some of the offences were related to culture - including the consumption of K-dramas and K-pop.
articles.titleJa: パンデミック中に北朝鮮での処刑が大幅に増加 - 報告書
articles.summary: KドラマやKポップの消費など、文化に関連する違反行為も含まれていた。

articles.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
articles.description: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.
articles.titleJa: インドネシアで発生した列車事故、生存者の救出に救助隊が急ぐ
articles.summary: 月曜日、インドネシアの首都近郊で2つの列車が衝突し、少なくとも15人が死亡した。

articles.title: What happened when Rebel Wilson gave evidence in court?
articles.description: The Hollywood star is accused by fellow Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes of making defamatory statements.
articles.titleJa: レベル・ウィルソンが法廷で証言したとき何が起こったのか？
articles.summary: ハリウッド女優は、同僚のオーストラリア人女優シャーロット・マッキンネスから名誉毀損的な発言があったと訴えられている。

articles.title: Babies among those tied up and allegedly abused in Indonesia childcare centre
articles.description: In a case that has gripped the nation, dozens of children have allegedly been mistreated at the centre in Yogyakarta.
articles.titleJa: インドネシアの保育センターで乳児らも拘束され虐待か
articles.summary: ヨギャカルタの保育センターで、数十人の子供たちが虐待された疑いがあるという事件が国民の関心を集めている。

articles.title: Canada commits CAD 500,000 to strengthen food safety and plant and animal health capacity
articles.description: Canada is providing CAD 500,000 (approximately CHF 286,000) to the Standards and Trade Development Facility (STDF) to strengthen sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) capacity of public and private sector stakeholders in developing and least-developed countries. The contribution will support STDF activities that help countries meet international food safety and plant and animal health standards and facilitate safe trade, based on the STDF Strategy for 2025-2030.
articles.titleJa: カナダ、食の安全と動植物の健康能力強化に50万カナダドルを拠出
articles.summary: カナダは、開発途上国などの食品安全と動植物の健康能力強化のため、50万カナダドルを拠出することを発表しました。

articles.title: WTO project aims to step up ePing use to increase transparency and market access in Africa
articles.description: Implementation of a WTO initiative to strengthen use of the ePing SPS & TBT Platform is now under way, helping governments, exporters and other stakeholders better track and engage in evolving product requirements affecting international trade. Funded by the Standards and Trade Development Facility (STDF), the three year project focuses on five African countries and aims to enhance transparency, predictability and market access.
articles.titleJa: WTOプロジェクト、アフリカでのePing活用促進で透明性と市場アクセス向上へ
articles.summary: WTOは、アフリカ5カ国でePingプラットフォームの活用を促進するプロジェクトを開始し、国際貿易における透明性と市場アクセス向上を目指します。

articles.title: India launches safeguard investigation on soda ash
articles.description: On 24 April 2026, India notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 16 March 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of soda ash.
articles.titleJa: インド、ソーダ灰に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: インドは、ソーダ灰の輸入に関するセーフガード調査を開始したとWTOに通報しました。

articles.title: Forty-six women-led businesses in Jordan to benefit from WEIDE digital trade programme
articles.description: Forty-six women-led businesses in Jordan have been selected as beneficiaries of the Women Exporters in the Digital Economy (WEIDE) Fund, allowing them to begin a year-long programme to strengthen their participation in international trade. The announcement was made in Amman on 23 April at a launch event hosted by the Jordan Enterprise Development Corporation (JEDCO), WEIDE's partner business support organization in the country.
articles.titleJa: ヨルダンの女性主導46社、WEIDEデジタル貿易プログラムの恩恵を受ける
articles.summary: ヨルダンの女性主導企業46社が、国際貿易への参加を強化するWEIDEプログラムの対象となりました。

articles.title: DG Okonjo-Iweala welcomes Chief Executive Sam Hou Fai of Macao, China to the WTO
articles.description: Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala met with the Chief Executive of Macao, China, Sam Hou Fai on 23 April at the WTO. They discussed the active role Macao, China plays at the WTO and its strong support for the multilateral trading system. As trade openness and transparency are key to Macao, China's strong economic performance, both leaders highlighted the importance of the WTO for supporting the ongoing contribution of Macao, China to the global economy.
articles.titleJa: オコンジョ＝イウェアラ事務局長、マカオ特別行政区の行政長官と会談
articles.summary: WTO事務局長は、マカオ特別行政区の行政長官と会談し、WTOにおけるマカオの役割と多角的貿易システムへの支援について議論しました。

articles.title: Canada launches safeguard investigation on certain wood goods
articles.description: On 23 April 2026, Canada notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 21 April 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of certain wood goods.
articles.titleJa: カナダ、一部木材製品に対するセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: カナダは、一部木材製品の輸入に関するセーフガード調査を開始したとWTOに通報しました。

articles.title: Ethiopia steps up domestic reforms to advance WTO accession negotiations
articles.description: Speaking at a meeting of the Working Party on Ethiopia's accession to the WTO held on 22 and 23 April, Ethiopia's Minister of Trade and Regional Integration and Chief Trade Negotiator, Kassahun Gofe Balami, informed members of several key decisions taken by the Ethiopian Government in response to members' requests. He reported that the negotiations have "reached a decisive juncture" and reiterated Ethiopia's commitment to engage constructively and substantively to advance and finalize the accession process.
articles.titleJa: エチオピア、WTO加盟交渉進展のため国内改革を強化
articles.summary: エチオピアは、WTO加盟交渉を加速させるための国内改革を強化しており、加盟国との建設的な関与を表明しています。

articles.title: WTO members discuss Aid for Trade, technical assistance, LDC priorities in Development Week
articles.description: WTO members examined Aid for Trade flows, the participation of least-developed countries (LDCs) in global trade and capacity-building for developing economies during WTO Development Week held from 20 to 22 April.
articles.titleJa: WTO、開発週間で貿易のための援助、技術支援、LDCの優先事項を議論
articles.summary: WTO開発週間では、貿易のための援助、最貧国（LDC）のグローバル貿易への参加、開発経済学のための能力構築について議論されました。

articles.title: Technology transfer workshop highlights role of enabling environment in LDCs
articles.description: WTO Deputy Director-General Xiangchen Zhang opened the WTO's workshop on incentives for technology transfer to least-developed countries (LDCs) on 21 April 2026, drawing attention to the conditions necessary for technology transfer to take root and deliver results in LDCs.
articles.titleJa: 技術移転ワークショップ、LDCにおける促進的環境の役割を強調
articles.summary: WTOは、最貧国（LDC）への技術移転を促進するためのワークショップを開催し、そのための条件の重要性を強調しました。

articles.title: Madagascar launches safeguard investigation on certain tableware, kitchenware, and household and packaging articles
articles.description: On 17 April 2026, Madagascar notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated on 15 April 2026 a safeguard investigation on imports of certain tableware, kitchenware, and household and packaging articles, of plastics.
articles.titleJa: マダガスカル、一部陶磁器・家庭用品のセーフガード調査を開始
articles.summary: マダガスカルは、一部のプラスチック製食器、調理器具、家庭用品・包装材の輸入に関するセーフガード調査を開始したとWTOに通報しました。
